what_happened: GitHub Actions workflow permissions audit: distinguish between workflow-level GITHUB_TOKEN permissions and GitHub App Token permissions
why: When a job uses actions/create-github-app-token to generate an App Token, the App Token's permissions are separately configured via permission-* parameters. The workflow-level GITHUB_TOKEN permissions (in the job's `permissions:` block) only govern what GITHUB_TOKEN itself can do — not what the App Token can do. The App Token is a different credential entirely.
fix_applied: Removed pull-requests: write from the plan job's GITHUB_TOKEN permissions block because the plan job never creates PRs using GITHUB_TOKEN. The App Token still has permission-pull-requests: write (needed to post comments and check PR status). Only the execute job creates PRs via GH_TOKEN (the App Token), not GITHUB_TOKEN.
pattern_to_remember: In GitHub Actions workflows using GitHub App Tokens: (1) workflow-level `permissions:` blocks scope the built-in GITHUB_TOKEN only, (2) actions/create-github-app-token `permission-*` parameters scope the App Token separately, (3) auditing permissions means checking both layers independently. A job can have narrow GITHUB_TOKEN permissions while the App Token has broader permissions for actual work.

what_happened: Workflow-level `permissions: {}` default-deny placement: goes between `on:` and `env:` blocks, or directly after `name:`
why: GitHub Actions documentation says workflow-level permissions can be placed at the top level (outside `jobs:`). Placing it between `on:` and `env:` is clean and visible.
fix_applied: Added `permissions: {}` after the `on:` block and before `env:` in symphony.yml
pattern_to_remember: For least-privilege GitHub Actions workflows: always add `permissions: {}` at the workflow level (before `jobs:`) as a default-deny, then explicitly grant permissions per-job. The `detect` job running only shell scripts needs `permissions: {}` explicitly since it inherits from workflow default.
